title: pilosella cymosa subsp. praealta
intro: Pilosella cymosa subsp. praealta, also known as Alpine Mouse-ear or Rock Mouse-ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to mountainous regions in Europe and Asia. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Pilosella cymosa subsp. praealta include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as potentially exerting antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, but more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential.
